Eurozone Bond Yields Open Marginally Higher — Market Talk
data.description: Eurozone government bond yields edge marginally higher after market opening, awaiting domestic and U.S. data. In the U.S., retail sales will be the main highlight after a strong March, while the PPI data will be watched after the cooler-than-expected CPI, says ING's rates strategist team. In the eurozone, industrial production data will be released alongside the second reading of first-quarter GDP growth. The 10-year German Bund yield is up 1 basis point at 2.694%, while the 10-year Italian-German yield spread remains at 100 basis points, the tightest level since 2021, according to Tradeweb. (emese.bartha@wsj.com)

data.title: Engie Backs View After Infrastructures Business Drives Earnings Growth
data.description:
(05:53 GMT) *Engie Backs 2025 View
(05:54 GMT) *Engie 1Q Rev EUR23.3B
(05:55 GMT) *Engie 1Q EBITDA EUR5.4B
(05:55 GMT) *Engie 1Q Ebitda Excl. Nuclear Power EUR4,9B
(05:55 GMT) *Engie 1Q Cash Flow From Operations EUR4.0B
(05:56 GMT) *Engie Sees 2025 Net Recurring Income Group Share Between EUR4.4B to EUR5.0B
(05:56 GMT) *Engie Sees 2025 EBIT Excl. Nuclear Between EUR8.0B to EUR9.0B
(05:57 GMT) *Engie: Backs Guidance Despite Uncertain Economic Environment
(06:31 GMT) Engie Backs View After Infrastructures Business Drives Earnings Growth

By Adam Whittaker

French power utility Engie confirmed its full-year guidance after earnings were boosted by its infrastructures business.

The company said Thursday that earnings before interest and taxes excluding nuclear power--a key company metric--rose 2.1% on an organic basis to 3.7 billion euros ($4.13 billion).

Engie said the rise was mainly attributable to a better performance in its infrastructures business. Within the division, EBIT from networks activities rose organically by 69% but declined organically by 19% for local energy infrastructures due to the normalization of market prices, which was expected, it said.

Earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortization were unchanged from the year prior at 5.4 billion euros.

Revenue increased 5.6% to 23.3 billion euros, while cash flow from operations--a measure of cash generated from normal business operations--declined 22% to 4 billion euros.

For 2025, the company continues to expect net recurring income group share between 4.4 billion and 5 billion euros as well as EBIT excluding earnings from nuclear power between 8 billion and 9 billion euros.

Write to Adam Whittaker at adam.whittaker@wsj.com

data.title: The proposal by the nomination committee of Q-linea AB (publ) for election of members of the board of directors
data.description:
The nomination committee of Q-linea AB (publ) submits the following proposals to be presented at the annual general meeting of shareholders to be held on June 26th, 2025, regarding the number and election of members of the board of directors.

The nomination committee proposes six members of the board of directors and no deputy members of the board of directors.

The nomination committee proposes re-election of Erika Kjellberg Eriksson, Mario Gualano, Karin Fischer and Jonas Jarvius as members of the board of directors, and election of Johan Bygge and Sebastian Backlund for a term of office until the end of the next annual general meeting. Furthermore, the nomination committee proposes election of Johan Bygge as chairman and re-election of Mario Gualano as vice chairman of the board.

Johan Bygge has a solid background in leading and developing prominent global companies, including CFO at Investor, Asia Head of EQT and senior executives at both Electrolux and Husqvarna. He currently sits on several boards, including: Tredje AP-fonden, Getinge AB (publ), Lantmännen ek för, CapMan Oyj, Scandi Standard AB (publ) and Guard Therapeutics International AB (publ). Johan's expertise in building companies for the long term and developing global market-leading companies makes him an optimal choice for chairman of Q-linea given the commercialization phase the company is in.

Sebastian Backlund is employed by Investment AB Öresund as Investment Manager and is expected to contribute to the work of the board and the company with his strong knowledge in financial and commercial analysis, internal structure and process improvement, capital market as well as M&A experience from his previous employers: MedCap AB (publ) and EY Corporate Finance. Sebastian currently sits on the board of Scandi Standard AB and has also served on the board of Multi-Ply Components. With Sebastian as an addition to the board, Q-linea gains access to additional knowledge and experience that are valuable for the journey towards becoming a profitable growth company.

Information about all the members proposed to Q-linea's board, the nomination committee's reasoned statement regarding the proposal to the board and the nomination committee's complete proposal will be available on the company's website, www.qlinea.com in connection with the issue of the notice to the 2025 annual general meeting, which is expected to take place during the week 21.

The nomination committee has been appointed according to the principles decided by the 2024 annual general meeting and consists of Öystein Engebretsen (Investment AB Öresund), chairman of the nomination committee, Erika Kjellberg Eriksson (Nexttobe AB, chairman of the board) and Ulf Landegren (Landegren Gene Technology AB).

data.title: National Grid's FY25 Profit Grows; Revenue Down
data.description:
National Grid (NG.L) said Thursday that its profit in fiscal 2025 rose, while revenue dropped year over year.

Profit attributable to equity shareholders of the parent for the 12 months ended March 31 was 2.90 billion pounds sterling, compared with 2.29 billion pounds a year ago. EPS moved to 0.614 pound from 0.571 pound.

The energy transmission and distribution company's revenue was 18.38 billion pounds, compared with 19.85 billion pounds earlier.

Meanwhile, the board proposed a final dividend of 0.3088 pound per share, payable on July 17 to shareholders of record on May 30. It paid 0.3912 pound per share a year ago. This takes the final dividend to 0.4672 pound per share, down from 0.5852 pound per share a year ago.

For fiscal 2026, the company anticipates an underlying EPS compound annual growth rate in the 6% to 8% range, compared with the fiscal 2025 baseline.

data.title: Mr Kipling-maker Premier Foods posts 6% rise in annual trading profit
data.description:
Britain's Premier Foods reported a 6% rise in annual trading profit on Thursday, helped by year-round growth in sales of its Mr Kipling and Nissin brands.

Premier Foods enjoyed a strong Christmas period, which, together with growth in a lower inflation environment in the first half, helped lift headline trading profit to 187.8 million pounds ($249.2 million) for the year ended March 29.

"Our premiumisation strategy continues to be highly relevant, reflecting the trend for consumers to trade up and treat themselves to ranges such as our Ambrosia Deluxe and Mr Kipling Signature Bites," CEO Alex Whitehouse said in a statement.

The strong sales growth seen by food producers is now threatened by U.S. President Donald Trump's trade policies and rising employer costs, which may prompt consumers to tighten their purse strings while they ride out the uncertainty.

Premier Foods expects revenue growth this year to be more equally balanced between volume and price-mix, with expectations for trading profit unchanged.

($1 = 0.7536 pounds)

data.title: UK food group Greencore seals food supplier Bakkavor deal
data.description:
Convenience food manufacturer Greencore said on Thursday it had agreed to the terms of a recommended acquisition of UK fresh food provider Bakkavor Group , in a deal valued at 1.2 billion pounds ($1.59 billion).

In April, the parties said they had reached an agreement in principle on the key financial terms of a possible cash and share offer by Greencore for Bakkavor.

The offer base consideration comprises of 0.604 new Greencore shares and 85 pence in cash, valuing each Bakkavor share at 200 pence, with potential for further value if there is a sale of Bakkavor's U.S. business, Greencore Group said on Thursday.

($1 = 0.7528 pounds)

data.title: UK Trade Deficit Falls in Three Months to March
data.description:
Britain's goods and services trade deficit in the three months to March shrank by 3.6 billion pounds sterling to 6.6 billion pounds, the Office for National Statistics said Thursday.

The UK's goods trade deficit fell by 4.3 billion pounds to 55.2 billion pounds, while the services trade surplus dropped by 700 million pounds to 48.6 billion pounds.

data.title: Qatar lowers July term price for al-Shaheen oil, sources say
data.description:
QatarEnergy has lowered the term price for al-Shaheen crude oil cargoes loading in July, setting it at a premium of $1.63 per barrel above Dubai quotes, trade sources said on Thursday.

The term price marked a decline from the premium of $1.76 per barrel set for June-loading cargoes.

The slight cut in the premium tracked similar decreases in Middle East benchmarks Dubai and Oman this month as more supply has come onto the market following the decision by the Organization of the Petroleum Exporting Countries and its allies, known as OPEC+, to hike output in May and June.

The producer sold cargoes to buyers including Unipec, Glencore and ExxonMobil, the sources added.

Separately, QatarEnergy also sold Qatar Land crude at a premium at above $2 a barrel to Unipec and Qatar Marine crude at a premium at $1.10 a barrel to PetroChina, they said.

data.title: Norway GDP Falls 0.1% in Q1
data.description:
The Norwegian economy shrank by 0.1% quarter-on-quarter in the first quarter of 2025, after a downwardly revised 0.9% drop in the previous quarter.

This marked the third consecutive quarter of contraction, although it was the mildest in the sequence.

The downturn was largely driven by continued decline in petroleum activities and ocean transport (-2% vs -1.7% in Q4).

Additionally, fixed investment dropped by 5.8%, reversing a 2% growth in the preceding quarter.

Government spending also rose at a slower pace (0.4% vs 0.5%).

Meanwhile, exports (-1.9 vs -0.3%) fell less than imports (-2.1% vs -0.1%).

On a more positive note, household consumption rebounded (1.5 vs -0.3%).

On a yearly basis, the GDP fell by 0.4% in the March quarter, following a 0.3% contraction in the prior period.

data.title: Rhythm Biosciences' Unit Partners With University of Melbourne for Breast Cancer Risk Assessment Study
data.description:
Rhythm Biosciences said its unit, geneType, secured a 12-month contract with the University of Melbourne to support a clinical study aimed at improving breast cancer screening through personalized risk assessment, according to a Thursday filing with the Australian bourse.

The study will fund additional imaging for high-risk participants, ensuring they receive necessary follow-up care at no personal cost, the filing said.

data.title: 3i Group Posts Higher Fiscal FY25 Attributable Profit, Gross Investment Return
data.description:
3i Group said Thursday its profit and gross investment return for fiscal 2025 rose year over year.

Profit attributable to shareholders for the 12 months ended March 31 was 5.04 billion pounds sterling, compared with 3.84 billion pounds earlier. EPS moved to 5.206 pounds from 3.967 pounds.

The British private equity and venture capital company's gross investment return was 5.06 billion pounds, compared with 3.88 billion pounds a year ago.

Meanwhile, the board proposed a second-half dividend of 0.425 pound per share, payable on July 25 to shareholders of record on June 20. It paid 0.345 pound per share a year ago. This takes the full-year dividend to 0.73 pound per share, up from 0.61 pound in the previous year.

Kirloskar Brothers Appoints CFO
data.description:
Fluid management products manufacturer Kirloskar Brothers has appointed Bhavesh Chheda as the chief financial officer of the company with effect from Wednesday, according to a same-day disclosure to the Indian stock exchanges.

Before joining the company, he was the CFO of Thermax Babcock & Wilcox Energy Solutions.

data.title: Storytel Group presents New Financial Targets and Guidance for 2025
data.description:
At today's Capital Markets Day in Stockholm, Bodil Eriksson Torp, CEO of Storytel Group, along with members of the executive management team, will update on the Group's strategic direction and operations, as well as present new financial targets 2028 and a guidance for the full year 2025.

Storytel Group’s financial targets 2028

The Board of Storytel Group has established the following financial targets:

  • Revenue CAGR to exceed 10 percent in constant currency rates (new target).
  • EBITDA margin to exceed 20 percent (new target).
  • Net debt/EBITDA (LTM) below 1.5x (new target).

Capital Allocation and Dividend Policy

When it comes to capital allocation, Storytel Group has a clear order of priorities. First, and foremost invest in organic growth in current ten core markets, secondly acquire Publishers and Streaming platforms in core markets, thirdly new market launches through acquisitions and organic expansion.

Storytel Group is expected to generate significant cash flow going forward. The Board will pursue a dividend policy that ensures a flexible capital allocation and returning excess liquidity to shareholders.

Guidance for the full year 2025 

  • Revenue growth of 7-10 percent in constant currency rates 
  • Adjusted EBITDA margin of 17.5-19.0 percent
  • Subscriber base growth of 10 percent
  • Operational capex below 5 percent of revenue

“Our focus on enriching more people's lives with stories remains and we will ensure that everyone can find a story, regardless of format. We have a solid financial position and are well-positioned for both organic and acquisition-driven growth across the Publishing segment and the Streaming segment. We are confident to deliver an EBITDA margin exceeding 20 percent, with a potential for even a higher steady state figure in the longer perspective, and we are committed to this with a balanced risk profile”, says Bodil Eriksson Torp, CEO Storytel Group.

Previous Financial Targets

Storytel Group’s previous mid-term targets was to reach a revenue of around 4.5 BSEK in 2026 with a streaming revenue CAGR of 10-12 percent, an adjusted EBITDA margin of above 15 percent, as well as operational cash flow of above 10 percent of revenue.

Today's Capital Markets Day starts at 14:00 CEST. Follow the live stream here: https://app.univid.io/session/67ee86cf412dd9d1af4f0b1c/register/

data.title: Market Chatter: China Temporarily Eases Trade Restrictions on US Firms
data.description:
China has suspended certain trade restrictions against 45 US entities, following both nations' agreement to reduce tariffs, Reuters reported Wednesday.

The 90-day pause affects 17 firms on China's "unreliable entities" list and 28 entities on export control lists, according to the report.

While restrictions are lifted, exporters must still apply for dual-use item shipments to the 28 controlled entities, Reuters wrote.
